title: Deep Wiki (agent-authored codebase wiki)
summary: Module 10 adds a DeepWiki-style hierarchical/mind-map explanation of a codebase. Coodra runs no LLM: the user's coding agent (Claude Code / Codex / Cursor) is the model, and Coodra ships the schema, the persistence, and the render (ADR-017; same thesis as ADR-012/013/015/016 — ship records and recipes, not a service). Two-pass, schema-first flow: a structure pass plans a WikiStructure (sections + pages with importance, relevant files, parentId hierarchy, diagram flag) persisted via wiki_save_structure; a content pass authors each page's Markdown (with Mermaid + code citations) via wiki_save_page; wiki_status drives progress/resume. Manifest 17 -> 20. Storage: wikis (structure envelope) + wiki_pages (per-page content/state), dual-dialect SQLite + Postgres, team-synced (push dispatch + team-rows-puller + handler enqueues) so a wiki authored on one machine renders cross-machine. CLI: coodra wiki generate/status/list/open/clean writes a grounding snapshot + an authoring recipe and scaffolds a deep-wiki-author Feature. Web: /wiki + /wiki/[id] render the mind-map nav + Markdown (react-markdown + remark-gfm) + Mermaid. Optional Graphify grounding (communities -> sections; god-nodes -> important pages). Deferred: the DeepWiki 'Ask the wiki' RAG chat.
